summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Zoom Integration feature. All 14 backend API tests passed. Frontend UI verified: Zoom Integration card visible for admin/teacher, shows 'Not Configured' amber status when no credentials, all existing settings sections (Display Name, Change Password, Join Group) still present. Previously tested features (WebSocket chat, video upload) verified working.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/zoom/status requires authentication (returns 403 without token)
Backend: GET /api/zoom/status returns {configured: false, connected: false} when no Zoom credentials
Backend: GET /api/zoom/connect requires authentication (returns 403 without token)
Backend: GET /api/zoom/connect returns 503 with 'not configured' message when no credentials
Backend: POST /api/zoom/disconnect requires authentication (returns 403 without token)
Backend: POST /api/zoom/disconnect succeeds even when not connected
Backend: POST /api/zoom/webhook rejects invalid JSON (returns 400)
Backend: POST /api/zoom/webhook handles Zoom CRC challenge (endpoint.url_validation) correctly
Backend: POST /api/zoom/webhook handles recording.completed with no connected teacher (returns no_connected_teacher)
Backend: POST /api/zoom/webhook ignores non-recording events (returns ignored)
Backend: Zoom webhook is public endpoint (no auth required for Zoom to call it)
Frontend: Settings page loads with data-testid='settings-page'
Frontend: Display Name section present with data-testid='settings-name-input'
Frontend: Change Password section present with data-testid='settings-current-pw-input'
Frontend: Join Group section present with data-testid='settings-join-code-input'
Frontend: Zoom Integration card visible for admin with data-testid='zoom-integration-card'
Frontend: 'Not Configured' status displayed with amber background when no credentials
Frontend: Helpful message about credentials shown
Frontend: Connect button NOT shown when Zoom is not configured (correct behavior)
Frontend: Disconnect button NOT shown when not connected (correct behavior)
Regression: Chat API still working (GET /api/chat returns messages)
Regression: Video upload endpoint still working (POST /api/lessons/{id}/recordings/upload returns 422 without file)
critical_code_review_comments: Zoom integration correctly handles 3 states: not configured, configured but not connected, connected
CRC challenge handler works independently of credentials (important for webhook validation)
Webhook endpoint is correctly public (no auth) since Zoom calls it directly
Frontend correctly shows Zoom card only for isTeacherOrAdmin users
Amber styling for 'Not Configured' state provides clear visual feedback
Helpful message explains that manual upload is still available when Zoom not configured
context_for_next_testing_agent: Zoom integration fully tested. Backend endpoints: GET /api/zoom/status (returns configured/connected status), GET /api/zoom/connect (starts OAuth, returns 503 when not configured), POST /api/zoom/disconnect (disconnects account), POST /api/zoom/webhook (handles CRC challenge and recording.completed events). Frontend Settings page shows Zoom Integration card for admin/teacher only with 3 states: Not Configured (amber), Configured but not connected (shows Connect button), Connected (shows green status with Disconnect button). Currently in 'Not Configured' state since ZOOM_CLIENT_ID and ZOOM_CLIENT_SECRET are empty in backend/.env.

zoom_connect.response_when_not_configured: 503 with detail 'Zoom integration is not configured yet'

zoom_webhook.crc_challenge: Returns plainToken and encryptedToken for endpoint.url_validation event
zoom_webhook.recording_completed: Auto-imports MP4 to teacher's most recent lesson
zoom_webhook.no_teacher: Returns status 'no_connected_teacher' when host_id not found

frontend_ui.connect_button: data-testid='zoom-connect-btn' (shown when configured but not connected)
frontend_ui.not_configured_state: Amber background with 'Not Configured' text and helpful message

mocked_api_note: Zoom API keys not configured (ZOOM_CLIENT_ID and ZOOM_CLIENT_SECRET empty in backend/.env). This is intentional per user request to 'build first, add keys later'. All Zoom OAuth flows will fail gracefully with 503 error, which is expected behavior.
